title: Jna-Yoga or the Denomination and the Real
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “The Supreme Lord said: 'When one understands that the world, this combination of matter and person, is based upon one and the same reality, one…” through “The inexhaustible soul, free from the modes, is pure, self-luminous and uncovered just like a fire, while the material body is like firewood…” through “My dearest, this spirit of wisdom [this transcendental intelligence] in its three states [of wakefulness, sleep and unconscious sleep],…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “That what was absent before, is absent afterwards and is not there [independently] in between, is but a designation, a reference. Whatever that…” through “Someone whose consciousness is fixed in the True Self does not give it a moment's thought whether he is standing, sitting, walking or lying…” through “Certain inauspicious matters one can overcome step by step by constantly thinking of Me [Vishnu-smarana], by [loudly or silently] honoring My…”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Some [yogis] keep their bodies under control and make them fit by focussing on health, employing different methods and exercises for the sake of…” through “Although the material body of someone regularly practicing yoga will attain fitness, being intelligent My devotee does not put faith in such…” through “The yogi who, free from desires, takes to My shelter and practices this process of yoga, experiences the inner happiness [of the soul] and…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच पर-स्वभाव-कर्माणि न प्रशंसेन् न गर्हयेत् । विश्वम् एकामकं पश्यन् प्रकृत्या पुरुषेण च
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca para-svabhāva-karmāṇi na praśaṃsen na garhayet / viśvam ekāmakaṃ paśyan prakṛtyā puruṣeṇa ca
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'When one understands that the world, this combination of matter and person, is based upon one and the same reality, one should refrain from praising and criticizing someone else's nature and activities.

verses.sanskrit: पर-स्वभाव-कर्माणि यः प्रशंसति निन्दति । स आशु भ्रश्यते स्वार्थाद् असत्य् अभिनिवेशतः
verses.transliteration: para-svabhāva-karmāṇi yaḥ praśaṃsati nindati / sa āśu bhraśyate svārthād asaty abhiniveśataḥ
verses.translation: He who praises or criticizes someone else's nature and actions quickly will stray from his [real] interest [of self-realization], because he thus adheres to the falsehood [of the world of opposites].

verses.sanskrit: तैजसे निद्रयापन्ने पिण्ड-स्थो नष्ट-चेतनः । मायां प्राप्नोति मृत्युं वा तद्वन् नानार्थ-दृक् पुमान्
verses.transliteration: taijase nidrayāpanne piṇḍa-stho naṣṭa-cetanaḥ / māyāṃ prāpnoti mṛtyuṃ vā tadvan nānārtha-dṛk pumān
verses.translation: A person aware of the objective diversity is just [as unaware of the one reality] as an embodied soul whose senses, overcome by sleep, experience the illusory reality [of a dream] or the deathlike state of having lost consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: किं भद्रं किम् अभद्रं वा द्वैतस्यावस्तुनः कियत् । वाचोदितं तद् अनृतं मनसा ध्यातम् एव च
verses.transliteration: kiṃ bhadraṃ kim abhadraṃ vā dvaitasyāvastunaḥ kiyat / vācoditaṃ tad anṛtaṃ manasā dhyātam eva ca
verses.translation: What would be good or what would be bad in this unreal, deceptive material duality that, considered by the mind and put into words, results in a false [insufficient] image of reality [*]?

verses.sanskrit: छाया-प्रत्याह्वयाभासा ह्य् असन्तो ऽप्य् अर्थ-कारिणः । एवं देहादयो भावा यच्छन्त्य् आ-मृत्युतो भयम्
verses.transliteration: chāyā-pratyāhvayābhāsā hy asanto 'py artha-kāriṇaḥ / evaṃ dehādayo bhāvā yacchanty ā-mṛtyuto bhayam
verses.translation: Even though shadows, echoes and mirages constitute mere suggestions, they create motives [in people]; the same way the body and what belongs to it gives rise to material conceptions [identifications] that create fear until the day one dies [for they offer no firm hold].

verses.sanskrit: आत्मैव तद् इदं विश्वं सृज्यते सृजति प्रभुः । त्रायते त्राति विश्वात्मा ह्रियते हरतीश्वरः
verses.transliteration: ātmaiva tad idaṃ viśvaṃ sṛjyate sṛjati prabhuḥ / trāyate trāti viśvātmā hriyate haratīśvaraḥ
verses.translation: The Master, the Lord, the Soul alone, manifests this universe and Himself, protects all that exists and Himself, and withdraws both the creation and Himself. Apart from the Soul, outside of Him, hence no separate living being can be found, just as no other basis can be found for this, within the Self perceived, threefold splendor of the gunas, that you must know as the threefold manifestation generated by the illusory energy of my [B.G. 14: 19].

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान् न ह्य् आत्मनो ऽन्यस्माद् अन्यो भावो निरूपितः । निरूपिते ऽयं त्रि-विधा निर्मूल भातिर् आत्मनि । इदं गुण-मयं विद्धि त्रि-विधं मायया कृतम्
verses.transliteration: tasmān na hy ātmano 'nyasmād anyo bhāvo nirūpitaḥ / nirūpite 'yaṃ tri-vidhā nirmūla bhātir ātmani / idaṃ guṇa-mayaṃ viddhi tri-vidhaṃ māyayā kṛtam
verses.translation: The Master, the Lord, the Soul alone, manifests this universe and Himself, protects all that exists and Himself, and withdraws both the creation and Himself. Apart from the Soul, outside of Him, hence no separate living being can be found, just as no other basis can be found for this, within the Self perceived, threefold splendor of the gunas, that you must know as the threefold manifestation generated by the illusory energy of my [B.G. 14: 19].

verses.sanskrit: एतद् विद्वान् मद्-उदितं ज्ञान-विज्ञान-नैपुणम् । न निन्दति न च स्तौति लोके चरति सूर्य-वत्
verses.transliteration: etad vidvān mad-uditaṃ jñāna-vijñāna-naipuṇam / na nindati na ca stauti loke carati sūrya-vat
verses.translation: Someone who, perfectly conversant and experienced, is well aware of what I have now described, does not blame or praise [in looking for another cause], and wanders the earth as free as the sun.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षेणानुमानेन निगमेनात्म-संविदा । आद्य्-अन्तवद् असज् ज्ञात्वा निःसङ्गो विचरेद् इह
verses.transliteration: pratyakṣeṇānumānena nigamenātma-saṃvidā / ādy-antavad asaj jñātvā niḥsaṅgo vicared iha
verses.translation: When one by direct perception, logical deduction, scriptural truth and one's self-realization knows that that what is inessential has a beginning and an end, one should move around in this world free from attachment [see also B.G. 2: 16].'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच नैवात्मनो न देहस्य संसृतिर् द्रष्टृ-दृश्ययोः । अनात्म-स्व-दृशोर् ईश कस्य स्याद् उपलभ्यते
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca naivātmano na dehasya saṃsṛtir draṣṭṛ-dṛśyayoḥ / anātma-sva-dṛśor īśa kasya syād upalabhyate
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Material existence is not the experience of the seer who is a soul of self-realization, nor the experience of the spiritless body that constitutes the seen. Whose experience might it be then, oh Lord?

verses.sanskrit: आत्माव्ययो ऽगुणः शुद्धः स्वयं-ज्योतिर् अनावृतः । अग्नि-वद् दारु-वद् अचिद् देहः कस्येह संसृतिः
verses.transliteration: ātmāvyayo 'guṇaḥ śuddhaḥ svayaṃ-jyotir anāvṛtaḥ / agni-vad dāru-vad acid dehaḥ kasyeha saṃsṛtiḥ
verses.translation: The inexhaustible soul, free from the modes, is pure, self-luminous and uncovered just like a fire, while the material body is like firewood that is without understanding. To which of the two belongs the experience of a material life in this world?'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच यावद् देहेन्द्रिय-प्राणैर् आत्मनः सन्निकर्षणम् । संसारः फलवांस् तावद् अपार्थो ऽप्य् अविवेकिनः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca yāvad dehendriya-prāṇair ātmanaḥ sannikarṣaṇam / saṃsāraḥ phalavāṃs tāvad apārtho 'py avivekinaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'As long as the soul is attracted to the body, the senses and the life force, material existence will continue to flourish, despite being meaningless to the ignorant.

verses.sanskrit: अर्थे ह्य् अविद्यमाने ऽपि संसृतिर् न निवर्तते । ध्यायतो विषयान् अस्य स्वप्ने ऽनर्थागमो यथा
verses.transliteration: arthe hy avidyamāne 'pi saṃsṛtir na nivartate / dhyāyato viṣayān asya svapne 'narthāgamo yathā
verses.translation: Even though not having a purpose of its own, the course of mundane existence [the 'wheel'] does not cease to exist; one keeps, like [being caught] in a dream, contemplating the objects of the senses and the meaninglessness associated with it [compare 3.27: 4, 4.29: 35 & 73, 11.22: 56, B.G. 2: 14].

verses.sanskrit: यथा ह्य् अप्रतिबुद्धस्य प्रस्वापो बह्व्-अनर्थ-भृत् । स एव प्रतिबुद्धस्य न वै मोहाय कल्पते
verses.transliteration: yathā hy apratibuddhasya prasvāpo bahv-anartha-bhṛt / sa eva pratibuddhasya na vai mohāya kalpate
verses.translation: But that [dreaming], presenting someone not awakened many undesirable experiences in his sleep, will certainly not confound the one who awakened.

verses.sanskrit: शोक-हर्ष-भय-क्रोध- लोभ-मोह-स्पृहादयः । अहङ्कारस्य दृश्यन्ते जन्म-मृत्युश् च नात्मनः
verses.transliteration: śoka-harṣa-bhaya-krodha- lobha-moha-spṛhādayaḥ / ahaṅkārasya dṛśyante janma-mṛtyuś ca nātmanaḥ
verses.translation: Lamentation, elation, fear, anger, greed, confusion, hankering and such, one observes with the birth and death of the false ego [ahankra] and [is] not [seen] with the soul [that does not take birth or die, see 11.22: 12, 11.23: 50-56, 11.25: 30].

verses.sanskrit: देहेन्द्रिय-प्राण-मनो-ऽभिमानो जीवो ऽन्तर्-आत्मा गुण-कर्म-मूर्तिः । सूत्रं महान् इत्य् उरुधेव गीतः संसार आधावति काल-तन्त्रः
verses.transliteration: dehendriya-prāṇa-mano-'bhimāno jīvo 'ntar-ātmā guṇa-karma-mūrtiḥ / sūtraṃ mahān ity urudheva gītaḥ saṃsāra ādhāvati kāla-tantraḥ
verses.translation: The individual soul [of identification] with his attention directed at the body, the senses, the life force and the mind, assumes, depending the gunas and the karma, his form within the [great universal] Self. With the lead [the stra] of the complete of nature thus very differently denominated [as a dog, ape or human being], he then, controlled by time, moves around in material existence.

verses.sanskrit: अमूलम् एतद् बहु-रूप-रूपितं मनो-वचः-प्राण-शरीर-कर्म । ज्ञानासिनोपासनया शितेन च्छित्त्वा मुनिर् गां विचरत्य् अतृष्णः
verses.transliteration: amūlam etad bahu-rūpa-rūpitaṃ mano-vacaḥ-prāṇa-śarīra-karma / jñānāsinopāsanayā śitena cchittvā munir gāṃ vicaraty atṛṣṇaḥ
verses.translation: A sober sage free from desire moving around in this world will, with the sword of transcendental knowledge sharpened by worship, cut with this, without a firm basis, [in false ego] being represented in the many forms of the mind, the speech, the life force and the bodily functions.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं विवेको निगमस् तपश् च प्रत्यक्षम् ऐतिह्यम् अथानुमानम् । आद्य्-अन्तयोर् अस्य यद् एव केवलं कालश् च हेतुश् च तद् एव मध्ये
verses.transliteration: jñānaṃ viveko nigamas tapaś ca pratyakṣam aitihyam athānumānam / ādy-antayor asya yad eva kevalaṃ kālaś ca hetuś ca tad eva madhye
verses.translation: Spiritual knowledge [jna, entails] the discrimination [of spirit and matter and is nourished by] scripture and penance, personal experience, historical accounts and logical inference. [It is based upon] that what is there in the beginning, what stays the same in between and what remains in the end of this [creation], namely the Time and Ultimate Cause [of brahman, the Absolute Truth, see also B.G. 10: 30 & 33, 11: 32 and kla].

verses.sanskrit: यथा हिरण्यं स्व्-अकृतं पुरस्तात् पश्चाच् च सर्वस्य हिरण्-मयस्य । तद् एव मध्ये व्यवहार्यमाणं नानापदेशैर् अहम् अस्य तद्वत्
verses.transliteration: yathā hiraṇyaṃ sv-akṛtaṃ purastāt paścāc ca sarvasya hiraṇ-mayasya / tad eva madhye vyavahāryamāṇaṃ nānāpadeśair aham asya tadvat
verses.translation: Just like gold alone being present before it is processed, is present when it is processed and is also present in the final product of the processing, I am present in different disguises of [the with the gunas processing of] this creation [see also guna-avatras].

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञानम् एतत् त्रिय्-अवस्थम् अङ्ग गुण-त्रयं कारण-कर्य-कर्तृ । समन्वयेन व्यतिरेकतश् च येनैव तुर्येण तद् एव सत्यम्
verses.transliteration: vijñānam etat triy-avastham aṅga guṇa-trayaṃ kāraṇa-karya-kartṛ / samanvayena vyatirekataś ca yenaiva turyeṇa tad eva satyam
verses.translation: My dearest, this spirit of wisdom [this transcendental intelligence] in its three states [of wakefulness, sleep and unconscious sleep], constitutes, with its manifestation in relation to the three natural basic qualities as the causing [of rajas], the caused [of tamas] and the causal agent [of sattva, compare 11.22: 30], the fourth factor [the 'gold' or turya] that as an independent variable stands for the single [Absolute] Truth of each of them.

verses.sanskrit: न यत् पुरस्ताद् उत यन् न पश्चान् मध्ये च तन् न व्यपदेश-मात्रम् । भूतं प्रसिद्धं च परेण यद् यत् तद् एव तत् स्याद् इति मे मनीषा
verses.transliteration: na yat purastād uta yan na paścān madhye ca tan na vyapadeśa-mātram / bhūtaṃ prasiddhaṃ ca pareṇa yad yat tad eva tat syād iti me manīṣā
verses.translation: That what was absent before, is absent afterwards and is not there [independently] in between, is but a designation, a reference. Whatever that was created and is known by something else, is actually only [a reference to] that something else - that is how I see it.

verses.sanskrit: अविद्यमानो ऽप्य् अवभासते यो वैकारिको राजस-सर्ग एसः । ब्रह्म स्वयं ज्योतिर् अतो विभाति ब्रह्मेन्द्रियार्थात्म-विकार-चित्रम्
verses.transliteration: avidyamāno 'py avabhāsate yo vaikāriko rājasa-sarga esaḥ / brahma svayaṃ jyotir ato vibhāti brahmendriyārthātma-vikāra-citram
verses.translation: Even though this creation, which appears with the transformations of the mode of passion, does not really exist, it thus shines forth as [a reference to] the Absolute Truth standing in its own light, as the Brahman [the impersonal form of God] of the variety of senses, their objects, the self and the transformations [of the five elements, see also siddhnta].

verses.sanskrit: एवं स्फुतं ब्रह्म-विवेक-हेतुभिः परापवादेन विशारदेन छित्त्वात्म-सन्देहम् उपारमेत स्वानन्द-तुष्टो ऽखिल-कामुकेभ्यः
verses.transliteration: evaṃ sphutaṃ brahma-viveka-hetubhiḥ $ parāpavādena viśāradena &chittvātma-sandeham upārameta % svānanda-tuṣṭo 'khila-kāmukebhyaḥ
verses.translation: When one by discriminating this way has achieved clarity about the Absolute of the Spiritual Truth, one must wisely speak against the opposite [of falsely identifying oneself], cut with the doubt regarding the Soul, and in the contentment of one's own spiritual happiness desist from all lusty [unregulated] matters [see B.G. 3: 34].

verses.sanskrit: नात्मा वपुः पार्थिवम् इन्द्रियाणि देवा ह्य् असुर् वायुर् जलम् हुताशः । मनो ऽन्न-मात्रं धिषणा च सत्त्वम् अहङ्कृतिः खं क्षितिर् अर्थ-साम्यम्
verses.transliteration: nātmā vapuḥ pārthivam indriyāṇi devā hy asur vāyur jalam hutāśaḥ / mano 'nna-mātraṃ dhiṣaṇā ca sattvam ahaṅkṛtiḥ khaṃ kṣitir artha-sāmyam
verses.translation: The body made of earth is not the true self, nor are the senses, their gods or the life air, the external air, water, fire or a mind only interested in matter ['food']; nor is the intelligence the true self, or material consciousness, the I that considers itself the doer, the ether, the earth, material things or the restraint [the universal primeval state of equilibrium].

verses.sanskrit: समाहितैः कः करणैर् गुणात्मभिर् गुणो भवेन् मत्-सुविविक्त-धाम्नः विक्षिप्यमाणैर् उत किं नु दूषणं घनैर् उपेतैर् विगतै रवेः किम्
verses.transliteration: samāhitaiḥ kaḥ karaṇair guṇātmabhir $ guṇo bhaven mat-suvivikta-dhāmnaḥ &vikṣipyamāṇair uta kiṃ nu dūṣaṇaṃ % ghanair upetair vigatai raveḥ kim
verses.translation: What would the merit be of someone who, by the meditative control of the senses - which are manifestations of the gunas -, properly has distinguished My abode [or personal identity]? Or whom would he blame when the senses are agitated? What difference would it make to the sun whether the clouds gather or disperse?

verses.sanskrit: यथा नभो वाय्व्-अनलाम्बु-भू-गुणैर् गतागतैर् वर्तु-गुणैर् न सज्जते यथा नभो वाय्व्-अनलाम्बु-भू-गुणैर् गतागतैर् वर्तु-गुणैर् न सज्जते तथाक्षरं सत्त्व-रजस्-तमो-मलैर् अहं-मतेः संसृति-हेतुभिः परम्
verses.transliteration: yathā nabho vāyv-analāmbu-bhū-guṇair $ gatāgatair vartu-guṇair na sajjate &yathā nabho vāyv-analāmbu-bhū-guṇair $ gatāgatair vartu-guṇair na sajjate &tathākṣaraṃ sattva-rajas-tamo-malair % ahaṃ-mateḥ saṃsṛti-hetubhiḥ param
verses.translation: Just as the sky itself is not affected by the coming and going qualities of the atmosphere, fire, water and earth, or by the qualities of the seasons [of heat and cold], also the Imperishable Supreme remains free from the influence of sattva, rajas and tamas, the modes at the foundation of the I-concept of material existence [see also 1.3: 36, 3.27: 1, B.G. 7: 13].

verses.sanskrit: तथापि सङ्गः परिवर्जनीयो गुणेषु माया-रचितेषु तावत् । मद्-भक्ति-योगेन दृढेन यावद् रजो निरस्येत मनः-कषायः
verses.transliteration: tathāpi saṅgaḥ parivarjanīyo guṇeṣu māyā-raciteṣu tāvat / mad-bhakti-yogena dṛḍhena yāvad rajo nirasyeta manaḥ-kaṣāyaḥ
verses.translation: One must nevertheless, by firm bhakti-yoga unto Me, ban all the impurity of passion from one's mind, until the contamination by the modes has been eliminated that was produced by the deluding material energy [see B.G. 7: 1 & 14 and **].

verses.sanskrit: यथामयो ऽसाधु चिकित्सितो नृणां पुनः पुनः सन्तुदति प्ररोहन् । एवं मनो ऽपक्व-कषाय-कर्म कुयोगिनं विध्यति सर्व-सङ्गम्
verses.transliteration: yathāmayo 'sādhu cikitsito nṛṇāṃ punaḥ punaḥ santudati prarohan / evaṃ mano 'pakva-kaṣāya-karma kuyoginaṃ vidhyati sarva-saṅgam
verses.translation: The same way a disease that was treated imperfectly time and again returns and gives a man trouble, also a mind not purified of its karmic contamination will torment an inexperienced yogi who is still of all kinds of attachment.

verses.sanskrit: कुयोगिनो ये विहितान्तरायैर् मनुष्य-भूतैस् त्रिदशोपसृष्टैः । ते प्राक्तनाभ्यास-बलेन भूयो युञ्जन्ति योगं न तु कर्म-तन्त्रम्
verses.transliteration: kuyogino ye vihitāntarāyair manuṣya-bhūtais tridaśopasṛṣṭaiḥ / te prāktanābhyāsa-balena bhūyo yuñjanti yogaṃ na tu karma-tantram
verses.translation: Imperfect yogis who are commanded by impediments in the form of the human beings [family members, disciples etc., see e.g. S'r S'r S'ikshshthaka-4] sent to them by the thirty gods [see tridas'a], will, by their perseverance in their previous life, once again [in a new life] engage in the practice of yoga, but never more be entangled in fruitful activities [see also 11.18: 14, B.G. 6: 41-42].

verses.sanskrit: करोति कर्म क्रियते च जन्तुः केनाप्य् असौ चोदित आ-निपतात् । न तत्र विद्वान् प्रकृतौ स्थितो ऽपि निवृत्त-तृष्णः स्व-सुखानुभूत्या
verses.transliteration: karoti karma kriyate ca jantuḥ kenāpy asau codita ā-nipatāt / na tatra vidvān prakṛtau sthito 'pi nivṛtta-tṛṣṇaḥ sva-sukhānubhūtyā
verses.translation: A normal living being affected by the work he performs, his karma, impelled by sometimes this and then again that impulse, remains in that position until the moment he dies. But someone intelligent is, despite being situated in the material position, not that [fickle], because he, with the experience of the happiness he found, gave up his material desire.

verses.sanskrit: तिष्ठन्तम् आसीनम् उत व्रजन्तं शयानम् उक्षन्तम् अदन्तम् अन्नम् । स्वभावम् अन्यत् किम् अपीहमानम् आत्मानम् आत्म-स्थ-मतिर् न वेद
verses.transliteration: tiṣṭhantam āsīnam uta vrajantaṃ śayānam ukṣantam adantam annam / svabhāvam anyat kim apīhamānam ātmānam ātma-stha-matir na veda
verses.translation: Someone whose consciousness is fixed in the True Self does not give it a moment's thought whether he is standing, sitting, walking or lying down, urinating, eating food or doing whatever else that manifests from his conditioned nature.

verses.sanskrit: यदि स्म पश्यत्य् असद्-इन्द्रियार्थं नानानुमानेन विरुद्धम् अन्यत् । न मन्यते वस्तुतया मनीषी स्वाप्नं यथोत्थाय तिरोदधानम्
verses.transliteration: yadi sma paśyaty asad-indriyārthaṃ nānānumānena viruddham anyat / na manyate vastutayā manīṣī svāpnaṃ yathotthāya tirodadhānam
verses.translation: When one is intelligent one does not take anything else [but the soul] for essential. Whenever such a one faces the not really [independently] existing matters of the senses, he from his logic denies them their separateness, so that they are like the things of a dream that disappear when one wakes up.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वं गृहीतं गुण-कर्म-चित्रम् अज्ञानम् आत्मन्य् अविविक्तम् अङ्ग । निवर्तते तत् पुनर् ईक्षयैव न गृह्यते नापि विसृय्य आत्मा
verses.transliteration: pūrvaṃ gṛhītaṃ guṇa-karma-citram ajñānam ātmany aviviktam aṅga / nivartate tat punar īkṣayaiva na gṛhyate nāpi visṛyya ātmā
verses.translation: The soul is not something one accepts or rejects [that comes and goes], but the ignorance [resulting in a material body] that you in many forms under the influence of karma and guna accepted as an inextricable part of yourself, My best one, [ultimately] dissolves again in simply that [spiritual] vision.

verses.sanskrit: यथा हि भानोर् उदयो नृ-चक्षुषां तमो निहन्यान् न तु सद् विधत्ते । एवं समीक्षा निपुणा सती मे हन्यात् तमिस्रं पुरुषस्य बुद्धेः
verses.transliteration: yathā hi bhānor udayo nṛ-cakṣuṣāṃ tamo nihanyān na tu sad vidhatte / evaṃ samīkṣā nipuṇā satī me hanyāt tamisraṃ puruṣasya buddheḥ
verses.translation: Just as the rising sun puts an end to the darkness in the human eye, without creating that what exists, also a clever and thorough search for My pure truth puts an end to the darkness of someone's intelligence.

verses.sanskrit: एष स्वयं-ज्योतिर् अजो ऽप्रमेयो महानुभूतिः सकलानुभूतिः । एको ऽद्वितीयो वचसां विरामे येनेषिता वाग्-असवश् चरन्ति
verses.transliteration: eṣa svayaṃ-jyotir ajo 'prameyo mahānubhūtiḥ sakalānubhūtiḥ / eko 'dvitīyo vacasāṃ virāme yeneṣitā vāg-asavaś caranti
verses.translation: This self-luminous, unborn, immeasurable Greatness of Understanding who is aware of everything, is the One Without a Second in whom words find their closure and, by whom impelled, speech and the life airs are moving.

verses.sanskrit: एतावान् आत्म-सम्मोहो यद् विकल्पस् तु केवले । आत्मन् ऋते स्वम् आत्मानम् अवलम्बो न यस्य हि
verses.transliteration: etāvān ātma-sammoho yad vikalpas tu kevale / ātman ṛte svam ātmānam avalambo na yasya hi
verses.translation: Whatever notion of duality one might have of the [Supreme] Soul is nothing but a figment of the mind; for such an idea there is no other ground than one's own [identified] self [compare 7.13: 7].

verses.sanskrit: यन् नामाकृतिभिर् ग्राह्यं पञ्च-वर्णम् अबाधितम् । व्यर्थेनाप्य् अर्थ-वादो ऽयं द्वयं पण्डित-मानिनाम्
verses.transliteration: yan nāmākṛtibhir grāhyaṃ pañca-varṇam abādhitam / vyarthenāpy artha-vādo 'yaṃ dvayaṃ paṇḍita-māninām
verses.translation: The explanations [in terms of good and bad, see also 11.21: 16] offered by the so-called scholars of the meaning of this, in names and forms perceivable, duality that unmistakably consists of the five elements, are [completely] in vain [see also 5.6: 11].

verses.sanskrit: योगिनो ऽपक्व-योगस्य युञ्जतः काय उत्थितैः । उपसर्गैर् विहन्येत तत्रायं विहितो विधिः
verses.transliteration: yogino 'pakva-yogasya yuñjataḥ kāya utthitaiḥ / upasargair vihanyeta tatrāyaṃ vihito vidhiḥ
verses.translation: The body of a yogi who with a lack of experience tries to engage in the practice of yoga, may be hindered by rising disturbances. In that case the following rule of conduct is prescribed.

verses.sanskrit: योग-धारणया कांश्चिद् आसनैर् धारणान्वितैः । तपो-मन्त्रौषधैः कांश्चिद् उपसर्गान् विनिर्दहेत्
verses.transliteration: yoga-dhāraṇayā kāṃścid āsanair dhāraṇānvitaiḥ / tapo-mantrauṣadhaiḥ kāṃścid upasargān vinirdahet
verses.translation: Some disturbances may be overcome by postures [sanas] combined with concentration exercises [dhrana], others can be defeated by penances [tapas, see ***], mantras and medicinal herbs.

verses.sanskrit: कांश्चिन् ममानुध्यानेन नाम-सङ्कीर्तनादिभिः । योगेश्वरानुवृत्त्या वा हन्याद् अशुभ-दान् शनैः
verses.transliteration: kāṃścin mamānudhyānena nāma-saṅkīrtanādibhiḥ / yogeśvarānuvṛttyā vā hanyād aśubha-dān śanaiḥ
verses.translation: Certain inauspicious matters one can overcome step by step by constantly thinking of Me [Vishnu-smarana], by [loudly or silently] honoring My names and such [japa, sankrtana], and by following in the footsteps of the masters of yoga [see also B.G. 6: 25].

verses.sanskrit: केचिद् देहम् इमं धीराः सु-कल्पं वयसि स्थिरम् । विधाय विविधोपायैर् अथ युञ्जन्ति सिद्धये
verses.transliteration: kecid deham imaṃ dhīrāḥ su-kalpaṃ vayasi sthiram / vidhāya vividhopāyair atha yuñjanti siddhaye
verses.translation: Some [yogis] keep their bodies under control and make them fit by focussing on health, employing different methods and exercises for the sake of material perfection [siddhis].

verses.sanskrit: न हि तत् कुशलादृत्यं तद्-आयासो ह्य् अपार्थकः । अन्तवत्त्वाच् छरीरस्य फलस्येव वनस्पतेः
verses.transliteration: na hi tat kuśalādṛtyaṃ tad-āyāso hy apārthakaḥ / antavattvāc charīrasya phalasyeva vanaspateḥ
verses.translation: It goes without saying that good health should not be worshiped when such an endeavor is useless [when one is very old e.g.], for in matters of the body one is subject to decay, just like a fruit on a tree [see also 11.15: 33].

verses.sanskrit: योगं निषेवतो नित्यं कायश् चेत् कल्पताम् इयात् । तच् छ्रद्दध्यान् न मतिमान् योगम् उत्सृज्य मत्-परः
verses.transliteration: yogaṃ niṣevato nityaṃ kāyaś cet kalpatām iyāt / tac chraddadhyān na matimān yogam utsṛjya mat-paraḥ
verses.translation: Although the material body of someone regularly practicing yoga will attain fitness, being intelligent My devotee does not put faith in such yoga and will [eventually] give it up [*4].

verses.sanskrit: योग-चर्याम् इमां योगी विचरन् मद्-अपाश्रयः । नान्तरायैर् विहन्येत निःस्पृहः स्व-सुखानुभूः
verses.transliteration: yoga-caryām imāṃ yogī vicaran mad-apāśrayaḥ / nāntarāyair vihanyeta niḥspṛhaḥ sva-sukhānubhūḥ
verses.translation: The yogi who, free from desires, takes to My shelter and practices this process of yoga, experiences the inner happiness [of the soul] and accepts no defeat by disturbances.'
